methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Qedma is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Asif Sinay, Netanel Lindner, and Dorit Aharonov, with $26M+ raised to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 comparable companies: 3 public (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting materiality thresholds—while 7 same-sector peers exist, none of the narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier) yielded the three or more datapoints required for a defensible median. A stage-progression method was therefore applied, using the $26M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A progression, yielding $52M. This single-method triangulation generated a range of $44M (low) to $60M (high), with a median of $52M.

The valuation supports a range of $44M–$60M, with $52M as the central estimate. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier for frontier-stage quantum hardware (market norms remain thin), and the absence of tight peer comparables at identical stage and valuation tier. A more robust cross-check would require either narrower peer data or additional valuation approaches tied to specific technical milestones or customer traction.
